title: "Need solution for viewing man pages; Garbled output"

### Post by biomech on 2009-09-12
Anyone have a quick answer to why I am receiving the garbled text below when trying to view man pages?  I am trying to view bash.1.gz in usr/share/man/man1.  My Linux+ text is from 2005.  It is asking for me to cat bash.1 and I receive "file does not exist " reply.  I have to cat bash.1.gz to return anything.

I am using Jaunty x64.  Thanks in advance to anyone who can help me clear up whatever mistakes I may be making!

### Post by NoaHall on 2009-09-12
Haha. You're trying to view a COMPRESSED FILE FORMAT as a text file :)
Instead, extract, and then view it.

---

### Post by biomech on 2009-09-12
> **NoaHall said:**
> Haha. You're trying to view a COMPRESSED FILE FORMAT as a text file :)
Instead, extract, and then view it.

I guess that's what I get for using a 2005 textbook.  Probably would've worked with the Fedora 2 distro it came with.  #-o

---

### Post by The Cog on 2009-09-12
The normal way to view man pages is with the **man** command, i.e. > man bash
Use **Q** to quit after reading. If for some reason the man command doesn't work, I guess this is about the best bet:> zcat /usr/share/man/man1/bash.1.gz | less

---

### Post by NoaHall on 2009-09-12
You're trying to view the manual for bash? Just do "man bash"

---

### Post by biomech on 2009-09-12
Thanks everyone!  Using man bash works perfect.  I'll have to use this experience when comparing commands with older/different distros to newer distros.

Thanks again!  :guitar:
PS.  It also would have helped if I had paid attention and read the instructions to unzip the file prior to moving forward.  Instead I looked over that step.  Genius!
